title: Aslia libanotica
taxonomy: Aslia libanotica is classified as a species of moth in the family Noctuidae, commonly known as the owlet moths or ear moths. It is a member of the subfamily Hadeninae, which is a group of nocturnal moths. This species is likely endemic to the Mediterranean region, given its specific name. The classification of Aslia libanotica is based on its physical characteristics and genetic makeup. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and distribution of this species.
